note: EU legislation — free reuse with attribution per Decision 2011/833/EU.

controls.title: Principles relating to processing of personal data
controls.description: Personal data shall be processed lawfully, fairly, transparently; collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes; adequate, relevant and limited; accurate; kept no longer than necessary; processed securely.

controls.title: Lawfulness of processing
controls.description: Processing is lawful only if at least one legal basis applies: consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, or legitimate interests.

controls.title: Processing of special categories of personal data
controls.description: Processing of sensitive data (health, biometric, racial origin, political opinion etc.) is prohibited unless an explicit exception applies.

controls.title: Information to be provided — data collected from the data subject
controls.description: At the time of collection, provide data subjects with identity of controller, purposes, legal basis, recipients, retention period, and data subject rights.

controls.title: Information to be provided — data not obtained from the data subject
controls.description: Where data is not collected directly from the data subject, provide equivalent notice within a reasonable period or at first communication.

controls.title: Right of access by the data subject
controls.description: Data subjects have the right to obtain confirmation of whether their personal data is being processed and to receive a copy, with details of the processing.

controls.title: Right to rectification
controls.description: Data subjects have the right to have inaccurate personal data corrected without undue delay.

controls.title: Right to erasure ('right to be forgotten')
controls.description: Data subjects have the right to erasure of personal data without undue delay in specified circumstances.

controls.title: Right to restriction of processing
controls.description: Data subjects have the right to obtain restriction of processing in specified circumstances (contested accuracy, unlawful processing, etc.).

controls.title: Right to data portability
controls.description: Data subjects have the right to receive their personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format and to transmit it to another controller.

controls.title: Right to object
controls.description: Data subjects have the right to object to processing based on legitimate interests, direct marketing, or research/statistics.

controls.title: Automated individual decision-making, including profiling
controls.description: Data subjects have the right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal or similarly significant effects.

controls.title: Data protection by design and by default
controls.description: Implement appropriate technical and organisational measures (e.g. pseudonymisation, data minimisation) both at the time of design and by default during processing.

controls.title: Processor
controls.description: Controllers may only use processors providing sufficient guarantees; must have a written contract specifying the subject-matter, nature, purpose, type of data, and obligations.

controls.title: Records of processing activities (RoPA)
controls.description: Controllers (and processors) must maintain records of processing activities including purposes, categories of data, recipients, international transfers, and retention periods.

controls.title: Security of processing
controls.description: Implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including encryption, pseudonymisation, confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience.

controls.title: Notification of a personal data breach to the supervisory authority
controls.description: In the case of a personal data breach, notify the supervisory authority without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after becoming aware.

controls.title: Communication of a personal data breach to the data subject
controls.description: When a breach is likely to result in a high risk to individuals, communicate the breach to the affected data subjects without undue delay.

controls.title: Data protection impact assessment (DPIA)
controls.description: Prior to processing that is likely to result in a high risk, carry out a DPIA. Required when using new technologies, large-scale processing of sensitive data, or systematic monitoring.

controls.title: Designation of the data protection officer (DPO)
controls.description: Designate a DPO when processing is carried out by a public authority, involves large-scale systematic monitoring, or large-scale processing of special categories or criminal conviction data.

controls.title: Position of the data protection officer
controls.description: The DPO shall be involved in all data protection matters, operate independently, report to the highest management level, and be accessible to data subjects.

controls.title: Tasks of the data protection officer
controls.description: The DPO's tasks include: informing and advising on obligations, monitoring compliance, cooperating with the supervisory authority, and acting as contact point.

controls.title: General principle for transfers
controls.description: Transfer of personal data to a third country may only take place if the conditions in Chapter V are complied with (adequacy decision, appropriate safeguards, or derogations).

controls.title: Transfers on the basis of an adequacy decision
controls.description: Transfer is permitted where the Commission has decided that the third country ensures an adequate level of protection.

controls.title: Transfers subject to appropriate safeguards
controls.description: In the absence of an adequacy decision, transfers may occur where appropriate safeguards are in place (e.g. Standard Contractual Clauses, Binding Corporate Rules, approved codes of conduct).
